fact: July 22 is exactly 203 days into the year, which means there are exactly 162 days left until New Year's Eve!

In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun has been baking the ground for weeks since the solstice, making July 22 one of the hottest days of the year even though the sun isn't at its highest point anymore.
Cicadas are typically screaming their loudest around July 22 in many places - male cicadas can make sounds up to 120 decibels, which is louder than a chainsaw!
July 22 falls right in the middle of Cancer season in astrology, making it the most 'Cancery' day of the year.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 22 is deep winter, and many eucalyptus trees are dropping their bark like giant snakes shedding their skin.

The sun rises earliest around June 21, but by July 22 in most northern places, sunrise is already happening about 30 minutes later - summer days are secretly getting shorter even though it feels hotter!
Fireflies are usually having their biggest light shows around this date in North America - they flash in specific patterns that are basically bug morse code.
July 22 is prime time for thunderstorms in many places because the super-heated air creates perfect conditions for those massive, towering storm clouds that look like giant cauliflower.
Many flowers like sunflowers and black-eyed Susans are typically at their most colorful around July 22, turning fields into giant yellow carpets that can be seen from airplanes.
In northern regions, July 22 still has nearly 16 hours of daylight, but the sun is setting with more orange and red colors than it did in June because of all the summer dust and humidity in the air.
Mosquitoes are often at their most active around this date in many places - a single female mosquito can lay up to 300 eggs every few days, and July heat makes them hatch super fast.

July 22 is when many lakes and rivers typically hit their warmest temperatures of the year, sometimes getting as warm as a bathtub - which is why algae blooms can turn water bright green like alien soup.
